description: Anti-spam tier evaluation test vectors for NOSTR Mail. The recipient's client evaluates each incoming message against a tiered policy to determine inbox placement.

tier_1.condition: Message includes valid Cashu P2PK token >= minimum sats

evaluation_rules: Tiers are evaluated in order from 0 to 2
The FIRST matching tier determines the result
A message that matches multiple tiers is classified at the LOWEST (highest priority) matching tier
Tier evaluation happens AFTER decryption (the client must unwrap the gift wrap first)
NIP-05 is used ONLY for address resolution, NOT as a trust signal
NIP-13 proof-of-work is NOT used in the anti-spam tier model

recipient_policy.description: The test recipient (Bob) has published this spam policy (kind 10097).

recipient_contacts.description: Bob's contact list (kind 3) — Alice is a contact, Charlie is NOT.
recipient_contacts.note: Alice is in Bob's contacts. Charlie (d3e4...d3e4) is NOT in Bob's contacts.

vectors.name: sender in contact list -> Tier 0 (FREE)
vectors.description: Alice sends a message to Bob. Alice is in Bob's kind 3 contact list.

expected.reason: Sender pubkey 2c7c...6748 found in recipient's kind 3 contact list.

vectors.name: valid Cashu P2PK token >= 10 sats -> Tier 1 (PAID)
vectors.description: An unknown sender attaches a Cashu P2PK token worth 21 sats, locked to Bob's pubkey.

expected.reason: Cashu P2PK token attached with 21 sats (>= 10 sat minimum). Token is locked to recipient's pubkey 98b3...3b2a and issued by trusted mint https://mint.example.com.

validation_steps: Parse the cashu tag value as a Cashu V4 token
Verify the token is from a mint the recipient trusts (matches accepted-mint in spam policy)
Verify the token total amount >= cashu-min-sats (10 sats)
Verify the P2PK lock pubkey matches the recipient's pubkey
Verify the token proofs are not already spent (call mint's /v1/checkstate endpoint)
If valid, redeem the token immediately to prevent double-spend

vectors.name: unknown sender, no payment -> Tier 2 (QUARANTINE)
vectors.description: A completely unknown sender with no payment. This is the default spam case.

expected.reason: Sender not in contacts and no valid Cashu postage. Message is quarantined per recipient's spam policy (unknown-action = quarantine).

vectors.note: Quarantined messages are stored but not shown in the inbox. The recipient can review the quarantine folder manually. The message is NOT rejected — the relay accepted it (gift wrap is valid), but the client classifies it as spam.

vectors.name: non-P2PK Cashu token -> Tier 2 (REJECTED)
vectors.description: Sender attaches a Cashu token that is NOT P2PK locked. Bearer tokens are rejected because they can be front-run by relays or network observers.

expected.reason: Cashu token is not P2PK locked. Bearer tokens are rejected to prevent front-running.

vectors.name: Cashu from untrusted mint -> Tier 2
vectors.description: Sender attaches a valid P2PK Cashu token but from a mint not in the recipient's accepted list.

expected.reason: Cashu mint is not in the recipient's accepted mints list.

vectors.name: contact + Cashu -> Tier 0 (contact wins)
vectors.description: Alice (in contacts) also attaches a Cashu token. Contact list is checked first, so the message is classified as Tier 0. The Cashu token SHOULD NOT be redeemed.

expected.reason: Sender is in contact list. Cashu token SHOULD NOT be redeemed.
expected.cashu_token_action: Do NOT redeem the Cashu token — the message qualified for free delivery via contact list.
